terms.definition: A LIFO (last-in, first-out) abstract data type with push/pop/peek operations on a single end.
A FIFO (first-in, first-out) abstract data type with enqueue at the back and dequeue from the front.
Last-In, First-Out: the most recently added item is the next one removed; defining order for a stack.
First-In, First-Out: items are removed in the order they were added; defining order for a queue.
Double-ended queue; in Python `collections.deque` supports O(1) add/remove at both ends.
A queue that dequeues the item with the highest (or lowest) priority instead of the oldest; typically implemented with a heap.
A flow-control technique where a slow consumer signals the producer to slow down — often implemented with a bounded queue.
